title: The Smithereens
description: Rediscover the hits that defined a generation., Alternative rock pioneers The Smithereens formed in New Jersey in 1980. Known for their high-energy performances and chart hits including “A Girl Like You” and “Blood and Roses,” they have earned enduring acclaim from fans and critics alike. The band continues to tour worldwide and captivate audiences with their signature guitar-driven sound., The Smithereens perform live in Grass Valley on Friday, July 24, 2026. Doors open at 7:00 PM and the show begins at 8:00 PM. Seats are limited, so secure your spot for a night of classic alternative rock.

title: Shamady - Comedy From Beyomedy
description: Shamady is an interactive comedy show and spiritual experience., Not sure if you want a night of laughter or a full-on spiritual breakthrough? You no longer have to decide. Shamady brings you both., You won't just watch this show, you'll get the chance to be part of it: stand-up comedy, guided meditations, and interactive healing ceremonies that are as funny as they are real. No experience, crystals, or prior enlightenment required., Your guides: stand-up comedian Aaron Weaver (Netflix is a Joke fest), improvisor Anne Walsh (Groundlings), Divine Source Energy (Hulu), and surprise special guests., The Universe is abundant. Tickets are not. Reserve your spot now., Tickets at www.elixart.com. Doors at 7pm, show at 8pm.

description: Cars and coffee grass valley is a weekly car event at daily donuts in the Mcknight crossing shopping center every saturday morning from 7 to 10

title: Paint a collaborative wall mural at Lucchesi Vineyards
description: A hands-on wall mural workshop will be offered at Lucchesi Vineyards in Grass Valley California. Gary Graham, a well-known muralist, will instruct the various procedures required to create a unique work of art. This workshop will allow you to participate in all stages of creating a wall mural, from design and drawing to the painting itself., Each day of this six-day workshop will focus on a different element of the wall mural creation. You can attend one day, a weekend, or all of the sessions. Each day is a separate ticketed event, so you can pick and choose for your schedule and interests., The workshop dates and topics are as follows:July 11 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 12 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 18 - Paint (underpainting), July 19 - Paint (underpainting), July 25 - Paint (overpainting), July 26 - Paint (overpainting + glazing)

title: Make your own Hand-Carved Wooden Spatula
description: Hand-Carved Wooden – Spoons and Spatulas (see tickets for project), 3 hours class – , There’s something deeply satisfying about shaping wood with your own two hands., In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll start with a simple block of wood and transform it into a beautiful, functional wooden utensil using traditional hand tools. Under expert guidance, you’ll learn how to safely and confidently use an axe, carving knife, and scoop knife to shape, hollow, and refine your piece., This class is perfect for anyone curious about traditional woodworking or wanting to slow down and experience the rhythm of working with hand tools. No prior experience is necessary — just bring your curiosity and a willingness to learn., By the end of class, you’ll leave with a hand-carved wooden spoon or spatula and the foundational skills to continue carving on your own., What You’ll Create , One hand-carved wooden spoon or spatula (depending on the class ticket you choose), What You’ll Learn, • Safe and proper use of an axe, • Knife carving techniques, • How to use a scoop knife for hollowing, • Foundational greenwood carving skills, Experience Level, Beginners— No experience needed, Ages, Adults, and Teens 13+ may participate when accompanied by an adult., (The accompanying adult may choose to observe or purchase a ticket to participate.), All materials and tools are provided.

title: Spanish story time
description: Monthly bilingual story time with Reyna Sanchez-Lopez. Enjoy lively books and activities to introduce children to the wonders of reading and books.

title: Saturday Get Behind the Wheel Ceramics
description: This workshop is for all levels (beginners welcome) who wish to develop and sharpen their pottery-throwing skills on the Potter’s Wheel. We will go over the basics of wedging, centering, pulling, and trimming but also cover how to throw bowls and plates. Everyone is guaranteed to leap a pot. Make that special pot that shows the sudden uptick in your skills. Enjoy playing with clay and making ceramic pottery with your own unique flair. All clay and materials included.

title: Pioneer Park Picnic Pops Concert
description: Under the direction of Cheryl Woldseth, the 60-member Nevada County Concert Band invites you to join us for the July Pioneer Park Picnic Pops Concert as part of our summer series of outdoor performances. Make it a family tradition to enjoy a picnic concert with the Nevada County Concert Band! Expect a lively program, including toe-tapping marches, iconic Broadway hits, contemporary tunes, and much more. Bring your friends, family, neighbors, and clubs to the park for a fun-filled evening of music with the official Nevada County band! The concert is free, open to the public, and accessible to all, making it a perfect outing for families. Leashed dogs are welcome. Pack a picnic dinner or treat yourself to hot dogs and ice cream from our vendors. Don't forget your lawn chair or blanket, and settle in for a delightful evening of music at the park.

title: 10,000 Maniacs
description: Celebrating 45 years as one of America’s most beloved alternative rock bands, 10,000 Maniacs bring to the stage a career-spanning show that blends classics and new music. From unforgettable hits like “These Are Days,” “Like the Weather,” “More Than This”, and “Because the Night, the band continues to inspire with poetic lyrics, lush melodies, and dynamic performances. , Fronted by Mary Ramsey and joined by founding members Jerome Augustyniak, John Lombardo, Dennis Drew, and Steven Gustafson, along with guitarist Ben Medina, 10,000 Maniacs invite you to experience a night of timeless music that has captivated audiences for generations.

title: The Wind Rises
venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
description: 7/26 - Jiro dreams of flying and designing beautiful airplanes, inspired by the famous Italian aeronautical designer Caproni. Nearsighted and unable to be a pilot, he becomes one of the world’s most accomplished airplane designers, experiencing key historical events in an epic tale of love, perseverance and the challenges of living and making choices in a turbulent world. A late career masterpiece from Director Hayao Miyazaki, THE WIND RISES is a spellbinding film beyond compare.

title: Stuffy Animal Adventures – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Bring your favorite Stuffy!, Step into the world of pretend play as we turn our classroom into a veterinarian’s office! Kids will give their stuffed animals check-ups, create their own doctor kits to take home, and learn how to care for their plush patients. Along the way, we’ll explore basic biology and anatomy through fun crafts, creative play, and hands-on activities.

title: Wonder Circle Mythic Art & Nature Journey Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: The Wonder Circle – Youth Maker XD Summer Camp, Some children see the world a little differently — they notice the light through the leaves, the shape of a shadow, the story hiding inside a stone. The Wonder Circle was made for them., Ages 9 to 12, 4 Day Camp | 3 hours a day, Monday – Thursday, 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM each day, $225, This small-group creative experience is designed for children aged 8–12 who are naturally curious, imaginative, and sensitive to the world around them. Unlike traditional art classes focused on technique or “how to draw,” The Wonder Circle invites children into a calm, story-rich environment where art, nature, and imagination work together to support creativity, emotional awareness, and confidence., Through painting, gathering natural materials, myth-inspired storytelling, and reflective creative practices, children explore their own ideas in ways that honor individuality rather than comparison. There are no right or wrong outcomes — only discovery., Each child is welcomed as a Wonderkeeper — a young creator learning to care for their imagination, their ideas, and their sense of belonging., This camp is perfect for the dreamer, the quiet observer, and the child who has always had something to say but hasn’t yet found the way to say it. Small group sizes ensure every Wonderkeeper gets the attention, space, and encouragement they deserve., Our camps are led by experienced instructors who are passionate about nurturing young creative minds. We take great care to create a welcoming, inclusive environment where every child feels valued, supported, and free to be exactly who they are., Class minimum 4 participants/ 6 maximum.

title: Mud Buddies! Adult & Child Ceramics Creations
description: Mud Buddies: Adult and Child or Teen Clay Explorations, Discover the delightful world of clay with our “Mud Buddies” workshop, a special two-session class designed for parents, caregivers, and children to explore the art of ceramics together. Join us and experience the joy of turning a chunk of clay into a beautiful and unique pottery piece., Session Details:Session 1: You and your child will get hands-on with clay, learning the basics of handbuilding techniques. Discover how to roll clay using a slab roller, design, cut, and impress a variety of textures into your creation for that personal touch. The first session ends with your pieces being set to bisque fire, preparing them for glazing., Session 2: The real fun begins as you return to your bisque-fired pieces and dive into the world of glazing. Choose from a range of colors and learn techniques to apply glaze, transforming your creations with vibrant finishes that you’ll treasure., Your ceramic pieces will be glaze fired and ready for pick up within approximate 10 days after your last class., What You Will Learn:Fundamental handbuilding techniques to shape and design clay., How to use a slab roller to prepare clay for designing., Various texturing techniques to personalize pottery pieces., The process of bisque firing and glazing ceramics., Key Information:Age Group: Suitable for Adults and children ages 8 + and teens., Dates & Times: Mondays, Ticket Price: $120 per pair (one adult and one child)., Class Size: Minimum of 3 pairs and a maximum of 5 pairs., Our experienced instructors are passionate about ceramic arts and dedicated to creating an inclusive, supportive, and fun environment. They are excited to share their skills and help foster a special bond as you and your child create together. Whether you’re looking to ignite a new hobby or simply spend quality time together, this workshop is the perfect blend of education and fun.

title: Ceramic Exploration – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Dive into the world of clay at our Creative Clay Creations Camp! Each session, students will explore the endless possibilities of stoneware clay to create functional and decorative pottery. With handbuilding techniques your child’s imagination truly has no bounds here., Throughout the week, our young artists will learn various techniques including extruding, slab building, texturizing, and pinch pottery. Under the guidance of an experienced potter, students will use underglazes and glazes to add vibrant colors and finishes to their creations. The camp is designed to foster creativity, improve fine motor skills, and introduce the fundamentals of ceramics in a fun, supportive environment.

title: Monday Night Ceramics – Pottery Wheel & Hand building
description: Engage your mind and come play with clay in this Class! In this comprehensive Ceramics and Pottery Class, we will explore both hand-building techniques and pottery wheel skills to create functional and non-functional ceramics and pottery projects. Join us at The Curious Forge and let your creativity flourish in our supportive and inspiring environment!

title: Alexander Technique for musicians
description: Learn to lessen tension while playing. Taught by a musician for musicians. Bring your instrument or voice. Paul McCartney, Sting, John Cleese, King Charles, Aldous Huxley and many others (including me on flute) have benefited from taking a dive into patterns of tension. We'll look at how you play, not what you play. I’ll help you observe and change. It’s not a quick fix but a new door may open. Register by texting or calling me at 530-798-9585, then address will be provided.

The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages.

title: Playwriting Group
description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

title: KVMR's Homegrown Storytellers
description: Community storytellers will weave true tales of their own Coming of Age, highlighting themes of personal transformation. , KVMR’s returning storytellers have brand new stories for the audience to enjoy, bringing fresh ideas and perspectives to the event. Storytellers include Lee Pope, Larry Hillberg, Tamara Luckinbill, Chris Towne, Michele Nesbit, Chuck Cropley, and David James., Under the guidance of KVMR broadcaster David James, this storytelling initiative has been continued since its inception in 2022. KVMR’s Homegrown Storytellers occurs twice a year as a seasonally themed call for local community members to try their skills at the oldest human cultural tradition of storytelling.

title: Hot Summer Nights Spring Street Stage Series
description: Free! This July during the popular Hot Summer Nights in Nevada City, The Miners Foundry and KVMR will present the Spring Street Stage, featuring live local music favorites on Wednesday nights, 6-9:00pm. Full bar outdoors and music all evening!

title: Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
description: Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use. Students are welcome to bring their own favorite brushes or watercolor set if desired., Skill Level / Prerequisites:, All skill levels welcome—from first-time painters to those continuing their watercolor journey., Class Length:, One 3-hour session, (Optional continuing classes offered monthly), Fee: $50 + $20 materials, Minimum 3 / Maximum 5 participants

title: Rockin' the Gateway presents "Journey Revisited"
description: The sounds of Journey fills the stage a Western Gateway Park on August 1. If you're a fan of the legendary band Journey, then you simply can't miss Journey Revisited's electrifying live performances! Founded in 2011, this California-based band has dedicated itself to bringing back the classic Journey sound, and boy, do they deliver! With their exceptional musicianship and raw talent, they've managed to recreate the iconic sound of the album-oriented rock era, without the need for any pre-recorded backing tracks or digital vocal enhancements., These guys cover all the fan favorites, from the ever-popular Don’t Stop Believin’ and Wheel in the Sky to Any Way You Want It, Lights, and many more. But what sets Journey Revisited apart from the rest is their sheer energy and passion for the music. They pour their hearts and souls into every performance, and it shows. The audience can't help but feel the electrifying energy that fills the room, and it's an experience that stays with you long after the show is over. So, if you're in the mood for some classic rock music that will take you on a journey through time, then make sure to catch Journey Revisited in action.

title: Woodturning 101 – Craft a Beautiful Wooden Bowl
description: Are you interested in woodturning and looking to learn a new skill? In this class, you’ll learn how to turn a beautiful bowl out of wood using a lathe. The process is both rewarding and relaxing, as you take a piece of raw wood and turn it into a functional work of wood art. Under the guidance of our experienced instructor, you’ll start by learning the basics of wood lathe operation and safety. Once you’re comfortable with the equipment, we’ll move on to selecting and preparing the wood for turning. With the wood secured on the lathe, you’ll use a series of specially designed tools to shape the bowl.

title: The Creative Labyrinth 101: Nature and Creativity Journal Making
description: Create a beautiful hard-cover journal or sketchbook from start to finish in this hands-on book arts workshop., Using the versatile Australian Hinge Binding, you’ll learn how to construct a professional-quality journal with a hard cover and multiple removable signatures. This unique binding style allows pages to be added, rearranged, or replaced over time, making it ideal for journaling, sketching, note-taking, nature observations, or creative projects., No previous bookbinding experience is needed. All materials and supplies are provided, and you’ll leave with a finished handcrafted book ready to use., What You’ll LearnThe anatomy of a hard-cover book, How to create and assemble book covers, Folding and preparing signatures, Australian Hinge Binding techniques, Methods for adding, removing, and rearranging pages, What You’ll CreateOne hard-cover journal, Ten removable signatures (approximately 40 pages), A versatile book that can be used as a journal, sketchbook, creative notebook, or nature journal, Optional Follow-Up Workshop, Participants may continue their journey in a second workshop, where the finished books become vessels for creative exploration through prompts, imagery, storytelling, and reflective practices inspired by nature and creativity., Class Details, Date: Sunday, August 2, Time: 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m., Ages: Adults & teens 13+, Skill Level: Beginner-friendly, Fee: $75, Class Size: Maximum 8 participants

title: Little Miss Sunshine
venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
description: 8/2 - An ensemble comedy masterpiece, The Onyx Downtown is excited to celebrate LITTLE MISS SUNSHINE for its 20th anniversary! The film follows the highly dysfunctional Hoover family as they travel 800 miles from Albuquerque, New Mexico to Redondo Beach, California, so their seven-year-old daughter Olive (Abigail Breslin) can compete in the "Little Miss Sunshine" children's beauty pageant. Along with Breslin, LITTLE MISS SUNSHINE boasts a cast that includes Toni Collette, Steve Carell, Greg Kinnear, and Alan Arkin. A critical darling and commercial success upon release, this road film still holds up today as a funny-yet-poignant examination of such themes as depression, self-acceptance, and finding meaning in the absurdity of life. Rated R.

Fans will enjoy a memorable evening of music that blends tradition and innovation.

Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., The Western Songbirds is a beloved acoustic trio based in the Nevada City and Grass Valley area, featuring singer-songwriters Sands Hall, Maggie McKaig, and Sally Ashcraft. title: And Then They Came for Us
description: AND THEN THEY CAME FOR US is a cautionary and inspiring documentary for all societies, directed by Abby Ginzberg and Ken Schneider and presented by Community Asian Theatre of the Sierra and The Onyx Theatre at the Nevada Theatre. In 1942, President Roosevelt signed Executive Order 9066, paving the way for the forced incarceration of 120,000 Japanese Americans. The film educates audiences about the constitutional damage done in the name of national security due to war hysteria and racism. Featuring actor George Takei and others who were incarcerated, rediscovered photos of Dorothea Lange and the story of Fred Korematsu’s long journey to justice, the film brings history into the present, as it follows Japanese Americans speaking out against the Muslim travel ban (the film was produced in 2017 at the height of the Muslim “hysteria”) A panel of internment camp survivors and descendants will talk with the audience afterwards and examine the current “immigrant hysteria” and the deportations of immigrants by ICE and other regressive immigration policies. The film represents an “eerie” undertone for the current times.

title: BBQ + Blues in Grass Valley
description: Church Street Concerts presents the Sid Morris Blues Trio on Wednesday, August 5, 2026, for a lively evening of BBQ and live blues in downtown Grass Valley., The Sid Morris Blues Trio knows the Blues. Three highly accomplished players will perform blues favorites with a sprinkling of classic American spirituals woven into the evening. The program is designed for blues fans, roots-music listeners, local residents, and anyone who enjoys strong musicianship in an intimate setting., BBQ will be served from 5:30-6:30 p.m. and is included with admission. The concert runs from 7:00-8:30 p.m. in the historic Grass Valley United Methodist Church sanctuary, a warm and welcoming venue with excellent acoustics., Tickets are $25 for adults and $5 for children age 12 and under. Tickets may be purchased online at ChurchStreetConcerts.com and will also be available at the door. The venue is located at 236 S. Church Street, Grass Valley, California.

title: Chris Dacre: INTER:reactions
venue_name: Center for the Arts | Granucci Gallery
description: Opening Reception: Friday., August 14, 2026 • 5-7pm, Exhibition Dates: August 6th- September 27th, 2026, Chris Dacre is a multi-disciplinary visual artist, educator and KVMRx radio deejay., These current artworks reflect three years of exploring painting as a medium. Moving between meticulous, reference-based painting and work created purely from imagination, I developed a visual dialogue across the pieces—a fragmented and often humorous exploration of the strange relationships between living beings and inanimate objects., From 2004 to 2017 I created work for my WAR is FUN! installations which transformed the heavy subject matter of war into large-scale, satirical environments influenced by my time in the U.S. Air Force. These works examine militarism, conflict, and the uneasy overlap between childhood play and adult warfare. I wanted to include some of that work in this exhibition because war continues to feel like a constant presence in our everyday lives.

title: Stand by Me, 40th Anniversary
venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
description: 8/9 - The Onyx Downtown is proud to present Growing Pains, a new series focused on highlighting some of the most powerful and profound coming-of-age films ever made. Based on a Stephen King novella, the film follows four young boys who embark on a two-day trek into the Oregon wilderness to find the dead body of a missing child. Directed by Rob Reiner and anchored by terrific performances from its four leads (Wil Wheaton, River Phoenix, Corey Feldman, and Jerry O’Connel), STAND BY ME stands as a timeless ode to youthful nostalgia and a legendary coming-of-age classic 40 years since its initial release. Rated R.

This theater company really does it all, building larger than life sets and puppets from cardboard, fabric, and papier-mache. Their shows are written to entertain and engage all ages, and showcase a uniquely in-person art form that’s hard to come across in our increasingly digital world. But it’s time to give the trusty tour bus, however comfy, a rest.

title: Playwriting Group
description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

title: The Creative Labyrinth 102: Filling the Pages
description: Session Two: prerequisite Creative Labyrinth 101, Bring your handmade journal to life in this playful and inspiring follow-up workshop., Using a combination of collage, simple book arts techniques, drawing, painting, writing, and collected imagery, you’ll begin transforming the blank pages of your Australian Hinge journal into a personal creative companion., Participants will explore themes and ideas through intuitive prompts, curated materials, and creative exercises designed to spark curiosity and imagination. Whether you’re creating a nature journal, visual diary, inspiration book, sketchbook, collection of favorite quotes, or something entirely your own, you’ll leave with new pages completed and plenty of ideas for continuing your book at home., No previous art experience is necessary. This workshop emphasizes exploration, experimentation, and creative play rather than artistic perfection., What You’ll LearnSimple methods for developing themes and creative direction, Collage techniques using paper, images, text, and found materials, Basic pocket-making techniques for storing notes, sketches, and treasures, Ways to combine writing, drawing, painting, and collected imagery on the page, Ideas for building an ongoing creative journaling practice, What You’ll CreateSeveral completed journal pages, Handmade pockets and interactive elements, A personalized framework for continuing your journal beyond the workshop, Class Details, Date: Sunday, August 16th, Time: 12:00 PM to 3:00 PM, Ages: Adults & teens 13+, Skill Level: Beginner-friendly, Fee: $75

title: Aftersun
venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
description: 8/16 - The Onyx Downtown is proud to present Growing Pains, a new series focused on highlighting some of the most powerful and profound coming-of-age films ever made. At a fading vacation resort, 11-year-old Sophie treasures rare time together with her loving and idealistic father, Calum (Paul Mescal). As a world of adolescence creeps into view, beyond her eye Calum struggles under the weight of life outside of fatherhood. Twenty years later, Sophie's tender recollections of their last holiday become a powerful and heartrending portrait of their relationship, as she tries to reconcile the father she knew with the man she didn't, in Charlotte Wells’ superb and searingly emotional debut film. Rated R.

title: Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
description: Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use. Students are welcome to bring their own favorite brushes or watercolor set if desired., Skill Level / Prerequisites:, All skill levels welcome—from first-time painters to those continuing their watercolor journey., Class Length:, One 3-hour session, (Optional continuing classes offered monthly), Fee: $50 + $20 materials, Minimum 3 / Maximum 5 participants

title: Pioneer Park Picnic Pops Concert
description: Under the direction of Cheryl Woldseth, the 60-member Nevada County Concert Band invites you to join us for our final performance of the Pioneer Park Picnic Pops summer series of outdoor concerts. Make it a family tradition to enjoy a picnic concert with the Nevada County Concert Band! Expect a lively program, including toe-tapping marches, iconic Broadway hits, contemporary tunes, and much more. Bring your friends, family, neighbors, and clubs to the park for a fun-filled evening of music with the official Nevada County band! The concert is free, open to the public, and accessible to all, making it a perfect outing for families. Leashed dogs are welcome. Pack a picnic dinner or treat yourself to hot dogs and ice cream from our vendors. Don't forget your lawn chair or blanket, and settle in for a delightful evening of music at the park.

title: Girls Town
venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
description: 8/23 - The Onyx Downtown is proud to present Growing Pains, a new series focused on highlighting some of the most powerful and profound coming-of-age films ever made. Mid-1990s, New York City. Patti, Angela, Emma and Nikki are high school seniors and best friends preparing for life beyond graduation. Their dreams for the future differ greatly – Angela wants to become a poet, Emma and Nikki have are heading for college, and Patti is a young mother looking for stability in her kid’s life – but the four bright, young women are steadfastly loyal to each other in a way only teenagers know how to be. Their lives, however, are completely and quickly upended by an unforeseen tragedy. Grieving, angry and forced to return to daily life without satisfying recourse for their feelings, the girls must come of age with a newfound understanding about the patriarchal world in which they live. Led by Lili Taylor, Bruklin Harris, Anna Grace and recent Academy Award-nominee Aunjanue Ellis-Taylor (KING RICHARD), the 1996 award-winning Sundance indie was co-written by the actors in collaboration with first-time filmmaker Jim McKay.
